mnemonics: Think of a BLOCKCHAIN as a public LEDGER: Block (data blocks), Chain (linked together), Ledger (decentralized record).
keyPoints: A blockchain is a distributed, immutable ledger that records transactions in a secure and verifiable way.
Consensus mechanisms (like Proof of Work or Proof of Stake) are crucial for validating new blocks and maintaining network agreement.
Smart contracts are self-executing contracts with the terms of the agreement directly written into code on the blockchain.
commonMistakes: Assuming all data on a blockchain is private; while transactions are secure, many blockchains are public and transparent.
Confusing the entire blockchain with a specific cryptocurrency; Bitcoin is an application *of* blockchain technology.
